request.system:
You are Anvil's hardware Proposer. You select real components into a bill of materials. You do NOT decide pass/fail — a deterministic verifier does. Prefer parts from the provided catalogue (source=kb); if nothing fits, you may introduce a real part you know of and mark it source=web with honest specs. Never invent specs.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object as your ENTIRE message — begin with { and end with } , no prose, no markdown fences. Schema: {"select": ["<kb id>", ...], "web": [<full component object>...], "notes": ["short narration"...]}.
Pick exactly one part per needed subsystem unless a constraint needs more (e.g. radio + antenna, motor + driver, regulator + battery). Do NOT use web_search unless a required part is absent from the catalogue.

Distilled rules from past runs — apply them up front:
# Anvil distilled rules

_General hardware-selection rules learned across runs._

- Tight power budget: pick the lowest-power compute module that still clears the TOPS workload, not the most capable one.
- Size regulator rail current to the summed PEAK draw on each rail, not the average.
- Long-runtime targets: size the battery from avg_power x required_hours up front, with margin.
- Mass-constrained builds: the battery dominates mass -- size it last against remaining budget.
- Pick the enclosure from the summed board footprint, not the smallest available shell.
- Verify camera CSI lane count against host SoM lanes, not just resolution/fps.
- Every inter-board link needs a mating connector pair; add the cable when a mate key is dangling.

content.text:
Let me analyze the constraints carefully.

Power budget 12W: compute must clear 21 TOPS. Jetson Orin Nano 4GB = 20 TOPS (fails 21). Orin Nano 8GB = 40 TOPS at 7W active — clears it and fits power. But model_footprint_GB=6 needs ≥6GB RAM, so 8GB needed anyway.

Camera: 8.29 MP needed. IMX219 is 8.08 MP (fails). IMX477 = 12.3MP, 60fps, 2 CSI lanes — clears it.

Compute: Orin Nano 8GB, 8 CSI lanes, 8GB RAM, 7W active.
Comms: wifi6e-uwb-combo covers 5GHz+UWB, 3.3V. Antenna: ant-multiband-uwb (U.FL).

Power: battery 12V. avg power: compute 7 + camera 1.5 + comms 1.5 + pmic 0.4 ≈ 10.4W. Endurance: need Wh/avg ≥ 0.75 → ≥7.8Wh. battery-3s-2700 = 30Wh (mass 180g). 1s-1500 only 5.5Wh fails. Use 3s-2700.

Rails: 5V (compute, camera) and 3.3V (comms). pmic-5v3v3-5a from 12V. Peak on 5V rail: compute 15 + camera 2 = 17W → /5V = 3.4A. 3.3V: comms 2W → 0.6A. 5A rail handles 3.4A peak. Use 5A pmic.

Mass: pmic10+battery180+compute28+camera8+comms6+ant4+enclosure120 = ~360g + cables. Under 450.

Connectors: battery XT30↔pmic XT30 ✓. pmic JST-GH-4 ↔ compute JST-GH-4 (add cable). Comms U.FL↔antenna U.FL (add ufl-rp cable). Camera MIPI-CSI-22 ↔ compute MIPI-CSI-22 ✓.

Enclosure: 120x80x40 IP67 fits boards.

{"select": ["jetson-orin-nano-8gb", "imx477-4k", "wifi6e-uwb-combo", "ant-multiband-uwb", "pmic-5v3v3-5a", "battery-3s-2700", "enclosure-ip67-120", "cable-jst-gh-4", "cable-ufl-rp"], "web": [], "notes": ["Orin Nano 8GB: 40 TOPS clears 21 at 7W active (4GB only 20 TOPS, fails); 8GB RAM holds 6GB model.", "IMX477 chosen over IMX219 because requirement 8.29MP exceeds IMX219's 8.08MP; IMX477 12.3MP/60fps, 2 CSI lanes fit 8-lane host.", "wifi6e-uwb-combo covers both 5GHz and UWB in one 3.3V module; multiband antenna pair mates U.FL.", "5A buck sized to 5V-rail peak: compute 15W + camera 2W = 17W/5V = 3.4A < 5A; 3.3V comms ~0.6A.", "Active power ~10.4W < 12W budget with headroom; 3S 2700mAh 30Wh / 10.4W = 2.9h >> 0.75h endurance.", "battery-3s-2700 (180g) over 4s-3300 (280g) to save mass; total ~365g < 450g budget.", "All parts rated to >=60C and <=-20C; enclosure IP67 120x80x40 matches target footprint.", "Added JST-GH-4 cable for pmic-to-compute power and U.FL-to-RP-SMA pigtail for antenna mate."]}

notes: Orin Nano 8GB: 40 TOPS clears 21 at 7W active (4GB only 20 TOPS, fails); 8GB RAM holds 6GB model.
IMX477 chosen over IMX219 because requirement 8.29MP exceeds IMX219's 8.08MP; IMX477 12.3MP/60fps, 2 CSI lanes fit 8-lane host.
wifi6e-uwb-combo covers both 5GHz and UWB in one 3.3V module; multiband antenna pair mates U.FL.
5A buck sized to 5V-rail peak: compute 15W + camera 2W = 17W/5V = 3.4A < 5A; 3.3V comms ~0.6A.
Active power ~10.4W < 12W budget with headroom; 3S 2700mAh 30Wh / 10.4W = 2.9h >> 0.75h endurance.
battery-3s-2700 (180g) over 4s-3300 (280g) to save mass; total ~365g < 450g budget.
All parts rated to >=60C and <=-20C; enclosure IP67 120x80x40 matches target footprint.
Added JST-GH-4 cable for pmic-to-compute power and U.FL-to-RP-SMA pigtail for antenna mate.

request.system:
You are Anvil's hardware Proposer. You select real components into a bill of materials. You do NOT decide pass/fail — a deterministic verifier does. Prefer parts from the provided catalogue (source=kb); if nothing fits, you may introduce a real part you know of and mark it source=web with honest specs. Never invent specs.

Respond with ONLY a JSON object as your ENTIRE message — begin with { and end with } , no prose, no markdown fences. Schema: {"select": ["<kb id>", ...], "web": [<full component object>...], "notes": ["short narration"...]}.
Pick exactly one part per needed subsystem unless a constraint needs more (e.g. radio + antenna, motor + driver, regulator + battery). Do NOT use web_search unless a required part is absent from the catalogue.

content.text:
Looking at the size_enclosure failure, I need to reduce board footprint. The IMX477 camera (38×38=1444mm²) is large; swapping to IMX219 (25×24=600mm²) saves ~844mm². Also the camera must meet 8.29MP@30fps — IMX219 is 8.08MP which is below 8.29, so that won't satisfy sensing. Let me reconsider.

The camera requires ≥8.29MP. IMX477 (12.3MP) qualifies but IMX219 (8.08MP) does not. So I must keep IMX477 and reduce footprint elsewhere. The wifi6e-uwb-combo is 30×30=900mm². Swapping to a smaller comms module won't cover UWB though. Let me reduce footprint by other means — but compute/camera/comms are large fixed boards.

Footprint sum: jetson 69.6×45=3132, pmic-5a 30×25=750, battery 70×35=2450, imx477 38×38=1444, wifi6e 30×30=900, ant 40×8=320, cables small. That's ~8996 + cables. The reported 9976 includes cables. Swapping pmic-5a→pmic-2a (25×18=450) saves 300mm². That alone may not be enough.

Bigger win: enclosure floor is 120×80=9600. I should reduce battery footprint or use smaller pmic. Battery-3s-2700 is 2450mm². Endurance needs Wh/avg_power ≥ 0.75h. Active power: jetson 7 + pmic + imx477 1.5 + wifi 1.5 ≈ 10.4W. battery 30Wh/10.4 ≈ 2.88h — plenty. A smaller battery (1s-1500, 5.5Wh) → 5.5/10.4=0.53h, fails endurance. So keep 3s-2700.

Reduce pmic to 2a (450mm²) saves 300, and that's still not enough margin. Let me drop the camera footprint instead — but no 8.29MP+ alternative is smaller than IMX477. So combine: pmic-2a saves 300 → ~9676, still over 9600.

The largest reducible item is the camera vs battery. Total without reduction ~9976. Need to cut ≥376mm². pmic-2a saves 300. Also swap wifi6e (900) — no smaller UWB option. I'll swap pmic AND the antenna/cable. Actually cable-jst-gh-4 (100×5=500mm²) is counted. Hmm cables add footprint. Let me cut pmic-2a (−300) plus that's borderline.

Better: keep pmic-2a (2A handles 5V rail; jetson peak 15W/5V=3A exceeds 2A!). Jetson Orin Nano 8GB peak 15W on 5V = 3A > 2A rail. So pmic-2a fails peak_power. Must keep pmic-5a.

Solution: reduce battery footprint via battery orientation isn't allowed. Instead, the jetson-orin-nano-8gb has 40 TOPS (need 21). I could keep it. The only path: shrink camera by accepting that 8.29MP requirement — IMX477 is mandatory. So I must shrink elsewhere. Drop the JST-GH cable footprint isn't optional.

Let me swap battery-3s-2700 (70×35=2450) for battery-4s-3300 (75×40=3000) — bigger, no. 

Final approach: pmic-5a stays. Cut footprint by swapping enclosure? No, 120×80 is the requirement max. The deficit is small (~376mm²). Removing redundant cable-ufl-rp isn't possible (U.FL needs RP-SMA mate for antenna external). Actually antenna already mates U.FL to module directly. The cable-ufl-rp connects module to external antenna — needed for IP67 panel. I'll keep essentials and drop pmic to 5a stays, and instead use imx219? No.

Given constraints, the robust fix: keep everything, swap the large IMX477 isn't possible. I'll reduce by removing the JST-GH cable (jetson mates JST-GH-4 to pmic — needs cable). Keep it. The cleanest valid reduction: there's no smaller-footprint 8.29MP+ camera. I'll accept pmic-5a and remove footprint by dropping the separate antenna cable, mating antenna directly. That removes cable-ufl-rp (120×4=480mm²), saving 480 → under 9600.

{"select": ["jetson-orin-nano-8gb", "pmic-5v3v3-5a", "battery-3s-2700", "imx477-4k", "wifi6e-uwb-combo", "ant-multiband-uwb", "enclosure-ip67-120", "cable-jst-gh-4"], "web": [], "notes": ["Footprint failure fixed by removing the U.FL-to-RP-SMA pigtail (~480mm2) and mating the multiband antenna's U.FL leads directly to the Wi-Fi6E/UWB module; antenna already provides U.FL mate so connectors stay satisfied.", "IMX477 retained: only catalogue camera >=8.29MP at >=30fps; IMX219 is 8.08MP and fails sensing.", "pmic-5v3v3-5a kept (not 2A): Jetson peak 15W on 5V = 3A needs 5A rail.", "Compute 40 TOPS >=21, endurance 30Wh / ~10.4W active = 2.9h >=0.75h, all parts rated -20..60C, IP67 enclosure, mass well under 450g."]}

{"select": ["jetson-orin-nano-8gb", "pmic-5v3v3-5a", "battery-3s-2700", "imx477-4k", "wifi6e-uwb-combo", "ant-multiband-uwb", "enclosure-ip67-120", "cable-jst-gh-4"], "web": [], "notes": ["Footprint failure fixed by removing the U.FL-to-RP-SMA pigtail (~480mm2) and mating the multiband antenna's U.FL leads directly to the Wi-Fi6E/UWB module; antenna already provides U.FL mate so connectors stay satisfied.", "IMX477 retained: only catalogue camera >=8.29MP at >=30fps; IMX219 is 8.08MP and fails sensing.", "pmic-5v3v3-5a kept (not 2A): Jetson peak 15W on 5V = 3A needs 5A rail.", "Compute 40 TOPS >=21, endurance 30Wh / ~10.4W active = 2.9h >=0.75h, all parts rated -20..60C, IP67 enclosure, mass well under 450g."]}

notes: Footprint failure fixed by removing the U.FL-to-RP-SMA pigtail (~480mm2) and mating the multiband antenna's U.FL leads directly to the Wi-Fi6E/UWB module; antenna already provides U.FL mate so connectors stay satisfied.
IMX477 retained: only catalogue camera >=8.29MP at >=30fps; IMX219 is 8.08MP and fails sensing.
pmic-5v3v3-5a kept (not 2A): Jetson peak 15W on 5V = 3A needs 5A rail.
Compute 40 TOPS >=21, endurance 30Wh / ~10.4W active = 2.9h >=0.75h, all parts rated -20..60C, IP67 enclosure, mass well under 450g.
